subject: Biology
title: The Microbiome Barrier
description: A fast breakdown of The Microbiome Barrier for builders.

hook.text: Trillions of friendly bacteria coat your skin and gut, and they fight invaders for you.

buildup.text: Your resident microbiome competes with pathogens for nutrients and attachment sites.

discovery.text: Gut bacteria produce antimicrobial compounds that kill harmful microbes trying to colonize.

twist.text: Antibiotics kill friendly bacteria too. That's why infections often follow antibiotic courses.

climax.text: A healthy microbiome is now considered a critical component of immune defense.

punchline.text: Your best allies are the trillions of microbes already living on you.

quiz.question: Why can antibiotics sometimes lead to new infections?
options: They kill protective friendly bacteria too
